frame_analyses.description: Initial setup showing laparoscopic trainer with Penrose drain and suture material visible, instruments positioned
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good starting position with instruments ready

frame_analyses.description: Trainee loading needle onto needle holder, preparing for suture placement
Placing suture through first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears accurate to the marked locations

frame_analyses.description: Continuing suture placement through second mark on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good positioning of suture through both marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot, creating initial loop
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Starting surgeon's knot formation with double throw

frame_analyses.description: Completing surgeon's knot double throw, pulling suture tight
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper surgeon's knot technique with secure double throw

frame_analyses.description: Performing second throw with hand switching, creating single throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good hand switching technique for second throw

frame_analyses.description: Executing third throw with proper hand switching from previous throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate final throw with hand switching maintained

frame_analyses.description: Cutting suture ends close to the knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper cutting technique leaving appropriate tail length

frame_analyses.description: Task completed with knot in place and suture ends cut
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result shows secure knot with drain slit well closed

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw completed
Single throw with proper hand switching from first throw
Final single throw with proper hand switching, knot appears secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit is well closed with no visible gap, knot properly positioned

technique_summary: Good overall technique with proper surgeon's knot formation, appropriate hand switching between throws, and accurate suture placement. Task completed efficiently within reasonable time frame.
improvement_suggestions: Work on more precise suture placement to minimize deviation from marks
Consider slightly faster needle loading to improve overall time
strengths: Excellent knot tying technique with proper surgeon's knot
Clean suture cutting with appropriate tail length
Secure final knot with complete drain closure
